summary: India operates Aadhaar, the world's largest biometric digital identity system, with over 1.3 billion enrolled individuals. Managed by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI), Aadhaar provides a 12-digit unique identity number linked to biometric and demographic data. It underpins access to government subsidies, banking, mobile services, and tax filing. The underlying technology is based on MOSIP (Modular Open Source Identity Platform), which has since been adopted by other countries.

sources.title: MOSIP - Modular Open Source Identity Platform

notes: Aadhaar is deeply integrated into India's digital public infrastructure stack (India Stack), alongside UPI for payments and DigiLocker for document storage. MOSIP is recognized as a Digital Public Good by the DPGA.
